publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2025-05-30 02:23:23 UTC.
profiledesc.langusage: Description is written in: Undetermined, Code for undetermined script script.

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Nesheim earned a B.S. in agricultural science, an M.S. in animal nutrition from the University of Illinois and his Ph.D. in nutrition from Cornell University. Professor emeritus in the Division of Nutritional Sciences and Provost Emeritus at Cornell University. Nesheim joined the faculty in 1959 and in 1974 he was named the director of the Division of Nutritional Sciences, a position that he held until 1987. Prior to becoming Cornell's provost in 1989, he held the position of Vice President for Planning and Budgeting. In 1995 he was elected to the American Academy of Arts and Sciences and in 1997 he was elected a fellow of the American Society of Nutritional Sciences.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Malden Nesheim Papers, #29-2-4272 . Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes biographical material, correspondence, records of the Dietary Guidelines Advisory Committee, the Cornell International Institute for Food, Agriculture, and Develpment (CIIFAD) and grant and conference files. Also includes drawings that form part of the "Survey of the Avian Alimentary" compiled by Professor Nesheim and David Crompton.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: See also collections #23-2-2817 and #47-10-3394 for additional biographical information.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Folder 9 in box 1 is restrcited until 2032, donor's request.
